steps.title: Eligibility
steps.intro: Confirm your project involves heritage and you're eligible to apply.

steps.title: Your Vision
steps.intro: What you want to achieve and the legacy you'll leave.

steps.title: Heritage Focus
steps.intro: Describe the heritage at the heart of your project.

steps.title: Project Plan
steps.intro: What you'll do, when, and how.

steps.title: Outcomes
steps.intro: How your project delivers against NLHF's programme outcomes.

steps.title: Budget
steps.intro: Cost breakdown, partnership funding, and VAT status.

elig.label: Is your project based in the UK?
What type of organisation are you?
What type of heritage does your project involve?
Total amount requested from the Heritage Fund
elig.help: This route covers grants between £10,000 and £250,000. No specific deadlines — decisions within 8 weeks.

vision.label: What does your organisation want to achieve through this project?
vision.help: Describe your vision and the hoped-for legacy. Max 150 words on the actual form.
vision.coachingTip: Think legacy, not logistics. Don't describe activities here — describe the CHANGE. What will be different in 5 years because this project happened?

vision.label: Have you received any advice from the Heritage Fund?
vision.help: If you used the Project Enquiry service, summarise the advice you received and how it influenced your project.
vision.coachingTip: Using the free Project Enquiry service before applying significantly improves your chances. If you haven't done this yet, consider pausing and requesting feedback first.

heritage.label: Describe the heritage as it is today
heritage.help: Include factual information. If physical: size, age, features, condition, importance, official recognition. If non-physical: subject area and time period.
heritage.coachingTip: Be a journalist here, not a marketer. State the facts: when was it built, what condition is it in, what records exist. The emotion comes later — first, establish the reality.

heritage.label: Is this heritage at risk? If so, how?
heritage.help: Threats could include physical damage, neglect, financial shortfall, loss of oral histories, restricted public access.
heritage.coachingTip: If the heritage IS at risk, this strengthens your case enormously. Be specific: 'The roof has been leaking since 2019 and 40% of the archive is water-damaged' is powerful. Don't exaggerate — but don't downplay either.

heritage.label: Why is this heritage important?
heritage.help: Who values it and why? Is it nationally/locally significant? Any designations (e.g., listed building, scheduled monument)?

project_plan.label: What will you do during your project?
project_plan.help: Overview of planned activities, events, restoration, conservation, or community engagement. Max 500 words on the actual form.
project_plan.coachingTip: Structure this clearly: Phase 1 (months 1-3): research and consultation. Phase 2 (months 4-8): delivery. Phase 3 (months 9-12): evaluation and legacy. Assessors skim — make it scannable.

project_plan.label: Who are your project partners?
project_plan.help: List any organisations, communities, or individuals you'll work with and their role.
project_plan.coachingTip: Named partners with confirmed commitment are gold. A letter of support from the local council or a museum director transforms 'we plan to work with' into 'we ARE working with'.

outcomes.label: How will your project save heritage?
outcomes.help: How will heritage be better managed, in better condition, or better understood?
outcomes.coachingTip: This is NLHF's #1 investment principle. Every project must make heritage more resilient. Even a community oral history project 'saves' heritage by recording it before it's lost.

outcomes.label: How will your project protect the environment?
outcomes.help: Consider sustainability in how you deliver the project, as well as environmental outcomes.

outcomes.label: How will you involve a wider range of people?
outcomes.help: All projects must demonstrate inclusion, access, and participation. Who will volunteer? Who will you reach that heritage currently doesn't?
outcomes.coachingTip: This is mandatory — every NLHF project must widen participation. Show you've thought about barriers: physical access, cost, language, digital access. Then show how you'll remove them.

outcomes.label: How will your organisation be more sustainable after this project?
outcomes.help: New skills, increased capacity, stronger partnerships, diversified income.

budget.label: Cost breakdown
budget.help: List each cost area: staff, materials, professional fees, equipment, contingency (10%), evaluation, access costs.
budget.coachingTip: Always include a 10% contingency — NLHF expects it. Include full cost recovery (your staff time, overheads). Don't ask for less than you need; underfunding kills projects.

budget.label: Partnership funding and other income
budget.help: What other funding have you secured or are applying for? Include cash and in-kind contributions.
budget.coachingTip: Having partnership funding — even 10-20% — dramatically strengthens your application. It shows other people believe in this project too.
